name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/13
units: metres below ground level; typed descriptions verbatim, depth = BASE of interval; OD/Reduced Level column used only to cross-check

intervals.verbatim: Stiff mottled orange brown and grey slightly sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium angular to subrounded gravel
Firm to stiff and stiff laminated brown with occasional grey mottle silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium angular gravel and occasional silt parting
Loose to medium dense brown silty fine SAND with occasional gravel of coal and occasional silty clay lamination
annotations: 'gravel of coal' = reworked coal clasts in glaciofluvial sand, not a coal seam

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense brown silty fine to coarse SAND with occasional fine angular gravel and occasional gravel of coal: becomes silty below 8.75m
annotations: internal note 'becomes silty below 8.75m' kept in verbatim

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense faintly laminated brown clayey sandy SILT with some black carbonaceous pockets some fine and medium gravel of coal occasional reddish brown silty clay lamination and some silty fine sand pockets
annotations: soft glaciolacustrine-type SILT (soil), classified drift not siltstone-bedrock
water: 'GWe' groundwater strike recorded at 9.90-10.00m on 22/6; Remarks: ground-water encountered overnight 19/6-22/6, level rose to 9.90m

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown silty fine to coarse SAND with occasional gravel of coal
Brown silty fine SAND with occasional fine angular gravel and occasional gravel of coal, occasional reddish brown silty clay lamination, and occasional pocket of fine and medium sand
Medium dense and dense brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional gravel of coal
annotations: bed spans the Sheet 2 / Sheet 3 break (continues past 20.00m)

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional gravel of coal and occasional silty clay lamination
Firm laminated brown very silty CLAY with some brown silty clay laminations occasional silt and fine and fine and medium sand pocket and occasional sandy silt pocket
annotations: text repeats 'fine and fine and medium' as written (likely clerk stutter, transcribed verbatim, not corrected)

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with some fine sand partings
Stiff orange brown and brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and cobbles and occasional brown silty clay pockets
annotations: chiselling 25.80-26.50m for 2.00h straddles this bed / bed 14

intervals.verbatim: Very stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and cobbles and occasional partings of silt and fine sand
annotations: chiselling 27.30-28.00m for 1.00h straddles this bed / bed 15

intervals.verbatim: Very dense brown clayey silty SAND with some brown sandy silty clay bands with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
Very stiff brown very sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and some clayey silty fine and medium sand pockets

notes: Wimpey Geotech supplementary ground investigation, A167 Durham Western Bypass, Borehole 1/13. Cable percussion (Pilcon Wayfarer), bored 19/6/92-24/6/92, logged/checked 28/6/92 (originator DAN, checked LKR); Lab Ref S/26671; Fig 14. 7 page images: pp01-03 = the borehole record Sheets 1-3 of 3 (30.00m, all Quaternary drift, rockhead NOT reached); pp04-07 = location/site plans (Site Plan (1)/(2)/(3) + General Layout, Roughton & Partners) with no strata. Whole sequence is superficial glacial/glaciofluvial/glaciolacustrine drift (topsoil, laminated clays, glaciofluvial sands, a glaciolacustrine silt, glacial till diamicton with cobbles at base); NO bedrock, NO coal seams (every 'gravel of coal' is reworked coal clasts). No pencil seam correlations present (hole does not reach Coal Measures); small pen squiggle symbols in the strata column near ~3.2m, ~24.2m and ~29.65m are draughtsman water/sample marks, not seams. Arithmetic clean: OD Level (Reduced Level) column checks 92.35 minus depth exactly at every boundary; no clerk slips. Water regime: groundwater encountered overnight 19/6-22/6, rose to 9.90m; GWe strike 9.90-10.00m (22/6); 'DRY' noted at ~27.00m (24/6) and ~28.75m. Chiselling runs 25.80-26.50m (2.00h), 27.30-28.00m (1.00h), 29.65-30.00m (1.50h) through the basal till. Location box gives site grid E 5897.7 N 6047.0; national-grid E/N 425892,541043 taken from task/header. Final depth 30.00m matches index.
